techniques.evidence_quote: a threat actor monitoring for and using exposed AWS keys for cryptomining operations
the threat actor associated with the campaign was able to create multiple AWS Elastic Compute (EC2) instances
used automated tools to continually clone public GitHub repositories and scan for exposed Amazon Web Services (AWS) IAM credentials
the actors began their reconnaissance operations using the AWS API DescribeRegions

techniques.evidence_quote: the threat actor creates AWS security groups ... before finally launching multiple EC2 instances

launching multiple EC2 instances per region across any accessible AWS region

techniques.evidence_quote: The downloaded payload was stored encrypted and then decrypted upon download

RunInstance API ... parameter for accepting AWS Cloud-Init scripts
the payload was stored in Google Drive

cryptojacking operations ... The threat actors used large-format cloud virtual machines ... specifically c5a.24xlarge AWS instances

attack_chain.description: Threat actor uses automated tools to continually clone public GitHub repositories and scan for exposed AWS IAM credentials in cleartext.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: used automated tools to continually clone public GitHub repositories and scan for exposed Amazon Web Services (AWS) IAM credentials

evidence_chunks.quote: used automated tools to continually clone public GitHub repositories and scan for exposed Amazon Web Services (AWS) IAM credentials
used automated tools to continually clone public GitHub repositories and scan for exposed Amazon Web Services (AWS) IAM credentials

attack_chain.description: Actor harvests the exposed AWS IAM access keys from GitHub commit/file within five minutes of exposure.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the actor was able to detect and use the exposed IAM credentials within five minutes of their initial exposure on GitHub

evidence_chunks.quote: the actor was able to detect and use the exposed IAM credentials within five minutes of their initial exposure on GitHub
the actor was able to detect and use the exposed IAM credentials within five minutes of their initial exposure on GitHub

attack_chain.description: Actor authenticates to AWS using the stolen IAM access keys, operating through a VPN to obscure identity.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the actor's automation operation is behind a VPN

attack_chain.description: Actor performs AWS reconnaissance starting with DescribeRegions, DescribeAccountAttributes, DescribeInstanceTypes, DescribeInstanceTypeOfferings to enumerate account and available regions.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: at 13:34:15, the actors began their reconnaissance operations using the AWS API DescribeRegions

evidence_chunks.quote: at 13:34:15, the actors began their reconnaissance operations using the AWS API DescribeRegions
at 13:34:15, the actors began their reconnaissance operations using the AWS API DescribeRegions

attack_chain.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Modify Cloud Compute Configurations
attack_chain.description: Actor creates/modifies AWS security groups to prepare for launching EC2 instances.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the threat actor creates AWS security groups ... before finally launching multiple EC2 instances per region

attack_chain.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Cloud Instance
attack_chain.description: Actor uses RunInstances API to launch multiple large-format c5a.24xlarge EC2 instances across multiple regions using private AMIs, more than 400 API calls in seven minutes.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: To instantiate Amazon EC2 instances, the threat actor used the RunInstance API

evidence_chunks.quote: To instantiate Amazon EC2 instances, the threat actor used the RunInstance API
To instantiate Amazon EC2 instances, the threat actor used the RunInstance API

attack_chain.description: Actor supplies Cloud-Init user data script to the RunInstances call so the EC2 instance auto-configures and fetches the mining payload at startup.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The Cloud-Init scripts are executed during the instance startup process. The threat actor used this mechanism to automate the EC2 instance configuration

evidence_chunks.quote: The Cloud-Init scripts are executed during the instance startup process. The threat actor used this mechanism to automate the EC2 instance configuration
The Cloud-Init scripts are executed during the instance startup process. The threat actor used this mechanism to automate the EC2 instance configuration

attack_chain.description: Instance downloads encrypted mining payload from a Google Drive URL (anonymous trusted web service used to deliver payload).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the payload was stored in Google Drive. Note that Google Drive URLs are anonymous by design

evidence_chunks.quote: the payload was stored in Google Drive. Note that Google Drive URLs are anonymous by design
the payload was stored in Google Drive. Note that Google Drive URLs are anonymous by design

attack_chain.technique_name: Obfuscated Files or Information: Encrypted/Encoded File
attack_chain.description: Downloaded payload is stored encrypted and decrypted on the instance prior to execution to evade detection.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: The downloaded payload was stored encrypted and then decrypted upon download

evidence_chunks.quote: The downloaded payload was stored encrypted and then decrypted upon download
The downloaded payload was stored encrypted and then decrypted upon download

attack_chain.description: Monero (XMR) miner 'appworker' runs on the EC2 instances, connecting to the SupportXMR pool (pool.supportxmr.com:443) to mine cryptocurrency at the victim's expense.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: 474 unique miners that were potentially actor-controlled Amazon EC2 instances ... mining pool that the threat actor used for this operation was the SupportXMR mining pool
